author: codex-agent

patch.value: Feature adds machine-checkable guardrails for low-signal fields (generic blocker text, generic unblock notes, placeholder repro steps, empty bodies) and emits actionable remediation output.
Extend pm validate with low-signal metadata quality checks
Add explicit validate diagnostics for placeholder-style metadata so governance quality regressions are detected before large cleanup sweeps are required.
pm validate can detect configured low-signal metadata patterns, report affected item IDs with rule names, and optionally fail CI gates when quality thresholds are exceeded.
Placeholder pattern catalog and expected diagnostic format are agreed with maintainers.
Shifts metadata quality from reactive cleanup to proactive validation
Reduces future governance drift and manual audit effort
Low-signal metadata is surfaced automatically during validation
This sweep identified hundreds of placeholder fields that were not visible in current validate checks.
Run pm validate on a tracker containing placeholder metadata and observe that current checks do not flag low-signal fields.
Teams gain earlier detection of tracker quality drift and lower remediation cost.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: pm search 'validate placeholder metadata blocked_reason unblock_note repro_steps' --limit 20; pm search 'External audit follow-up: validation and large-output ergonomics' --limit 20; pm list-open --limit 20. Net-new because existing validation items focus on structural checks,not semantic low-signal metadata detection.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Checks should be configurable and avoid false positives by supporting per-rule opt-outs and rule versioning.

message: Create feature for low-signal metadata validation checks
